fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Hawaii Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Hawaii - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity hawaii, how to transfer occupational therapist license to hawaii, occupational therapist compact hawaii, hawaii occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Hawaii DCCA Professional & Vocational Licensing — OT
board.parent_dept: Hawaii Department of Health (DOH) Office of Health Care Assurance

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Hawaii.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Graduate from an Accredited OT Program
steps.description: Complete an occupational therapy educational program accredited by the Accreditation Council for Occupational Therapy Education (ACOTE).

steps.title: Pass the NBCOT Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination.

steps.title: Submit an Application
steps.description: Complete and submit an application for licensure to the Hawaii Board of Occupational Therapy, including all required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Hawaii OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Hawaii have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers occupational therapist practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Hawaii?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Hawaii
hero.dek: Hawaii Occupational Therapist license requires graduation from an ACOTE accredited program, passing the NBCOT exam, and completing the application process.
